title: corydalis chionophila subsp. parviflora
intro: Corydalis chionophila subsp. parviflora, also known as the Snow Corydalis or small-flowered snow fumewort, is a type of flowering plant in the poppy family, Papaveraceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, making it beneficial for relieving pain and reducing inflammation. The health benefits of Corydalis chionophila subsp. parviflora include its ability to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and muscle spasms, as well as its potential to improve cognitive function and reduce stress. In herbal preparations, Corydalis chionophila subsp. parviflora is typically combined with other herbs, such as passionflower and valerian root, to create a calming tea or tincture.
